# AI Tarot Reading Page Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Add an interactive AI-powered 3-card tarot reading page to the LUX static site, with card reveal animation, ambient music, and readings generated by Groq through a Google Apps Script proxy.

**Architecture:** Pure static HTML/CSS/JS (no build step), matching the existing site. Card data and pure draw logic live in ES modules importable by both the browser and Node's built-in test runner. The Groq API key stays server-side in Google Apps Script Script Properties; the browser calls only the existing `/exec` URL using a CORS-safe `text/plain` POST.

**Tech Stack:** Vanilla HTML/CSS/ES modules, HTML5 `<audio>`, Google Apps Script (`UrlFetchApp`), Groq (`llama-3.3-70b-versatile`), Node built-in test runner (`node:test`) for dev-time unit tests only.

## Global Constraints

- No build step, no bundler, no npm runtime dependencies. Shipped site is hand-written HTML/CSS/JS.
- No new third-party services beyond Groq. Proxy reuses the existing Google Apps Script; hosting stays GitHub Pages.
- Groq API key MUST live only in Apps Script Script Properties (`GROQ_API_KEY`). It must never appear in any browser-served file.
- Browser → Apps Script requests use `Content-Type: text/plain` with **no other custom headers** (avoids CORS preflight so the response is readable). Body is JSON text.
- Palette tokens (reuse from `style.css`): `--parchment #F2E8D5`, `--oxblood #6B1A1A`, `--gold #C9A84C`, `--ink #1A1412`. Fonts: Playfair Display (display) + Inter (UI).
- Deck images already exist at `design/assets/cards/<index> <Name>.jpg`, indices 0–77, standard Rider-Waite order. Card back is `design/assets/cards/card-back.svg`. Audio is `design/assets/The Shadow of the Old Grove.mp3`.
- Existing waitlist files (`index.html`, `style.css`, `main.js`) must keep working. Node test files are dev-only and not required by the shipped page.

---

### Task 1: Deck data model (`deck.js`)

**Files:**
- Create: `deck.js`
- Test: `test/deck.test.mjs`

**Interfaces:**
- Produces: `export const DECK` — an array of 78 objects, each `{ id: number, name: string, image: string, upright: string, reversed: string }`, ordered by `id` 0–77. `image` is the repo-relative path `design/assets/cards/<id> <name>.jpg`.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**

Create `test/deck.test.mjs`:

```js
import { test } from 'node:test';
import assert from 'node:assert/strict';
import { DECK } from '../deck.js';

test('deck has 78 unique cards ordered 0..77', () => {
  assert.equal(DECK.length, 78);
  DECK.forEach((c, i) => assert.equal(c.id, i));
  assert.equal(new Set(DECK.map(c => c.name)).size, 78);
});

test('every card has meanings and a correct image path', () => {
  for (const c of DECK) {
    assert.ok(c.upright.length > 0, `${c.name} upright`);
    assert.ok(c.reversed.length > 0, `${c.name} reversed`);
    assert.equal(c.image, `design/assets/cards/${c.id} ${c.name}.jpg`);
  }
});

test('key cards sit at their canonical indices', () => {
  assert.equal(DECK[0].name, 'The Fool');
  assert.equal(DECK[19].name, 'The Sun');
  assert.equal(DECK[22].name, 'Ace of Wands');
  assert.equal(DECK[77].name, 'King of Pentacles');
});
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run test to verify it fails**

Run: `node --test test/deck.test.mjs`

```js

export const DECK = CARDS.map(([name, upright, reversed], id) => ({
  id,
  name,
  image: `design/assets/cards/${id} ${name}.jpg`,
  upright,
  reversed,
}));
```

### Task 2: Pure draw logic (`draw.js`)

**Files:**
- Create: `draw.js`
- Test: `test/draw.test.mjs`

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: `DECK` from `deck.js` (array of card objects).
- Produces:
  - `export function shuffle(array, rng = Math.random)` → new array, Fisher–Yates shuffled; does not mutate input.
  - `export const POSITIONS = ['Past', 'Present', 'Future']`
  - `export function drawSpread(deck, rng = Math.random)` → array of exactly 3 objects `{ card, orientation, position }`, where `card` is a deck entry, `orientation` is `'upright'` or `'reversed'`, and `position` is `POSITIONS[i]`. The 3 cards are distinct.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**

Create `test/draw.test.mjs`:

```js
import { test } from 'node:test';
import assert from 'node:assert/strict';
import { shuffle, drawSpread, POSITIONS } from '../draw.js';
import { DECK } from '../deck.js';

// Deterministic rng that cycles through given values.
const seq = (values) => { let i = 0; return () => values[i++ % values.length]; };

test('shuffle does not mutate input and keeps all elements', () => {
  const input = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
  const out = shuffle(input, seq([0.1, 0.5, 0.9, 0.3]));
  assert.deepEqual(input, [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]);
  assert.deepEqual([...out].sort(), [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]);
});

test('drawSpread returns 3 distinct cards with valid positions', () => {
  const spread = drawSpread(DECK, seq([0.42]));
  assert.equal(spread.length, 3);
  assert.deepEqual(spread.map(s => s.position), POSITIONS);
  const ids = spread.map(s => s.card.id);
  assert.equal(new Set(ids).size, 3);
});

test('orientation is always upright or reversed, over many draws', () => {
  for (let i = 0; i < 200; i++) {
    const spread = drawSpread(DECK);
    for (const s of spread) {
      assert.ok(s.orientation === 'upright' || s.orientation === 'reversed');
      assert.ok(s.card && typeof s.card.name === 'string');
    }
  }
});

test('reversed orientation is chosen when rng >= 0.5', () => {
  // First 3 rng calls pick cards (shuffle), then 3 calls pick orientation.
  const spread = drawSpread(DECK, seq([0, 0, 0, 0.9, 0.9, 0.9]));
  assert.ok(spread.every(s => s.orientation === 'reversed'));
});
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run test to verify it fails**

Run: `node --test test/draw.test.mjs`
Expected: FAIL — cannot find module `../draw.js`.

- [ ] **Step 3: Write minimal implementation**

Create `draw.js`. Orientation is chosen after the shuffle so the test's rng sequencing is predictable:

```js
export const POSITIONS = ['Past', 'Present', 'Future'];

export function shuffle(array, rng = Math.random) {
  const a = [...array];
  for (let i = a.length - 1; i > 0; i--) {
    const j = Math.floor(rng() * (i + 1));
    [a[i], a[j]] = [a[j], a[i]];
  }
  return a;
}

export function drawSpread(deck, rng = Math.random) {
  const picked = shuffle(deck, rng).slice(0, 3);
  return picked.map((card, i) => ({
    card,
    orientation: rng() >= 0.5 ? 'reversed' : 'upright',
    position: POSITIONS[i],
  }));
}
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Run test to verify it passes**

Run: `node --test test/draw.test.mjs`
Expected: PASS (4 tests).

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add draw.js test/draw.test.mjs
git commit -m "feat: add pure 3-card draw logic with unit tests"
```

### Task 3: Reading page markup and styles (`reading.html`, `reading.css`)

**Files:**
- Create: `reading.html`
- Create: `reading.css`

**Interfaces:**
- Produces DOM element IDs consumed by `reading.js` in later tasks: `#question`, `#draw-btn`, `#spread`, `#reading`, `#reading-status`, `#retry-btn`, `#sound-toggle`, `#bg-audio`. `#spread` contains three `.card-slot` elements, each with a `.card` (holding `.card-face.card-back`, `.card-face.card-front > img`) and a `.card-position` label.

- [ ] **Step 1: Create `reading.html`**

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>LUX — Your Reading</title>
  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
  <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@300;400&family=Playfair+Display:ital,wght@0,400;0,700;0,900;1,400&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
  <link rel="icon" type="image/jpeg" href="design/assets/19 The Sun.jpg">
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="reading.css">
</head>
<body>
  <div class="bg" aria-hidden="true"></div>

  <button id="sound-toggle" type="button" class="sound-toggle" aria-pressed="false"
          aria-label="Toggle background music">♪ off</button>
  <audio id="bg-audio" src="design/assets/The Shadow of the Old Grove.mp3" loop preload="auto"></audio>

  <main class="reading-page">
    <header class="reading-head">
      <p class="wordmark">LUX</p>
      <h1 class="headline">Ask, and the cards will answer.</h1>
    </header>

    <form id="ask-form" class="ask-form" novalidate>
      <label for="question" class="form-label">What weighs on you?</label>
      <textarea id="question" name="question" rows="2"
                placeholder="Name your question, or simply draw…"></textarea>
      <button id="draw-btn" type="submit">DRAW YOUR SPREAD →</button>
    </form>

    <section id="spread" class="spread" aria-live="polite" hidden>
      <div class="card-slot">
        <div class="card"><div class="card-face card-back"></div><div class="card-face card-front"><img alt=""></div></div>
        <p class="card-position"></p>
      </div>
      <div class="card-slot">
        <div class="card"><div class="card-face card-back"></div><div class="card-face card-front"><img alt=""></div></div>
        <p class="card-position"></p>
      </div>
      <div class="card-slot">
        <div class="card"><div class="card-face card-back"></div><div class="card-face card-front"><img alt=""></div></div>
        <p class="card-position"></p>
      </div>
    </section>

    <section class="reading-zone" hidden>
      <p id="reading-status" class="reading-status" role="status"></p>
      <div id="reading" class="reading"></div>
      <button id="retry-btn" type="button" class="retry-btn" hidden>Ask the cards again</button>
    </section>
  </main>

  <script type="module" src="reading.js"></script>
</body>
</html>
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create `reading.css`**

```css
*, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }

:root {
  --parchment: #F2E8D5;
  --oxblood: #6B1A1A;
  --gold: #C9A84C;
  --ink: #1A1412;
  --ease-out: cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94);
}

html, body { min-height: 100%; }
body {
  font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif;
  background: var(--ink);
  color: var(--parchment);
  overflow-x: hidden;
}
.bg {
  position: fixed; inset: 0; z-index: 0;
  background: url('design/assets/background.png') center / cover no-repeat;
  opacity: 0.5;
}

.reading-page {
  position: relative; z-index: 1;
  max-width: 760px; margin: 0 auto;
  padding: 4rem 1.25rem 5rem;
  display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 2.5rem;
}
.wordmark { font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif; letter-spacing: 0.4em; color: var(--gold); font-size: 0.9rem; }
.headline { font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif; font-weight: 900; font-size: clamp(2rem, 7vw, 3.25rem); line-height: 1.05; margin-top: 0.5rem; }

.ask-form { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.9rem; }
.form-label { color: var(--gold); font-size: 0.85rem; letter-spacing: 0.05em; }
#question {
  background: rgba(242, 232, 213, 0.06); border: 1px solid rgba(201, 168, 76, 0.4);
  color: var(--parchment); font-family: inherit; font-size: 1rem;
  padding: 0.9rem 1rem; border-radius: 4px; resize: vertical;
}
#question::placeholder { color: rgba(242, 232, 213, 0.4); }
#draw-btn, .retry-btn {
  align-self: flex-start; cursor: pointer;
  background: var(--oxblood); color: var(--parchment);
  border: 1px solid var(--gold); font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif;
  letter-spacing: 0.12em; padding: 0.85rem 1.6rem; border-radius: 4px;
  transition: background 0.25s var(--ease-out), transform 0.25s var(--ease-out);
}
#draw-btn:hover, .retry-btn:hover { background: #7f2020; transform: translateY(-2px); }
#draw-btn:disabled { opacity: 0.55; cursor: wait; transform: none; }

/* Spread */
.spread { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); gap: 1rem; }
.card-slot { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 0.6rem; }
.card {
  position: relative; width: 100%; aspect-ratio: 300 / 520;
  transform-style: preserve-3d; transition: transform 0.7s var(--ease-out);
}
.card.revealed { transform: rotateY(180deg); }
.card-face {
  position: absolute; inset: 0; backface-visibility: hidden;
  border-radius: 8px; overflow: hidden;
  border: 1px solid rgba(201, 168, 76, 0.5);
}
.card-back { background: url('design/assets/cards/card-back.svg') center / cover no-repeat; }
.card-front { transform: rotateY(180deg); background: var(--ink); }
.card-front img { width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: cover; display: block; }
.card-front.reversed img { transform: rotate(180deg); }
.card-position { font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif; color: var(--gold); font-size: 0.8rem; letter-spacing: 0.15em; text-transform: uppercase; }

/* Reading */
.reading-zone { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 1rem; }
.reading-status { color: var(--gold); font-style: italic; min-height: 1.2em; }
.reading { font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1.7; white-space: pre-wrap; }
.reading p { margin-bottom: 0.9rem; }
.retry-btn { align-self: center; }

/* Sound toggle */
.sound-toggle {
  position: fixed; top: 1rem; right: 1rem; z-index: 3; cursor: pointer;
  background: rgba(26, 20, 18, 0.7); color: var(--gold);
  border: 1px solid var(--gold); border-radius: 999px;
  font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 0.8rem; letter-spacing: 0.05em;
  padding: 0.45rem 0.9rem;
}

@media (max-width: 520px) {
  .spread { gap: 0.5rem; }
  .card-position { font-size: 0.65rem; }
}

@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  .card { transition: none; }
  #draw-btn, .retry-btn { transition: none; }
}
```

### Task 4: Draw-and-reveal interaction (`reading.js`, no AI yet)

**Files:**
- Create: `reading.js`

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: `DECK` from `deck.js`; `drawSpread` from `draw.js`; DOM IDs from Task 3.
- Produces: module-scope helpers reused by later tasks — `renderSpread(spread)` populates the three card slots and triggers the flip; `currentSpread` holds the last drawn spread array. On submit, prevents default, draws, reveals cards, and (for now) shows the fallback meanings text.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write `reading.js`**

```js
import { DECK } from './deck.js';
import { drawSpread } from './draw.js';

const form = document.getElementById('ask-form');
const drawBtn = document.getElementById('draw-btn');
const questionEl = document.getElementById('question');
const spreadEl = document.getElementById('spread');
const readingZone = document.querySelector('.reading-zone');
const readingEl = document.getElementById('reading');
const statusEl = document.getElementById('reading-status');

let currentSpread = [];

function fallbackText(spread) {
  return spread
    .map(s => `${s.position} — ${s.card.name}${s.orientation === 'reversed' ? ' (reversed)' : ''}: ` +
              `${s.orientation === 'reversed' ? s.card.reversed : s.card.upright}`)
    .join('\n\n');
}

function renderSpread(spread) {
  const slots = spreadEl.querySelectorAll('.card-slot');
  spreadEl.hidden = false;
  slots.forEach((slot, i) => {
    const { card, orientation, position } = spread[i];
    const cardEl = slot.querySelector('.card');
    const front = slot.querySelector('.card-front');
    const img = slot.querySelector('.card-front img');
    front.classList.toggle('reversed', orientation === 'reversed');
    img.src = card.image;                       // browser encodes the spaces in the path
    img.alt = `${card.name}${orientation === 'reversed' ? ', reversed' : ''}`;
    slot.querySelector('.card-position').textContent = position;
    cardEl.classList.remove('revealed');
    // Stagger the flip so cards reveal one after another.
    setTimeout(() => cardEl.classList.add('revealed'), 200 + i * 260);
  });
}

form.addEventListener('submit', (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  currentSpread = drawSpread(DECK);
  renderSpread(currentSpread);
  readingZone.hidden = false;
  statusEl.textContent = '';
  readingEl.textContent = fallbackText(currentSpread);
});
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Manual QA**

Run: reload `http://localhost:8000/reading.html`, type a question, click **Draw**.
Expected: three cards appear face-down and flip one-by-one to reveal card images; reversed cards are upside-down; position labels read Past / Present / Future; the fallback meanings text appears below. Drawing again reshuffles.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add reading.js
git commit -m "feat: draw and reveal a 3-card spread with fallback meanings"
```

### Task 5: Ambient audio and mute toggle

**Files:**
- Modify: `reading.js`

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: `#bg-audio`, `#sound-toggle` from Task 3; the existing submit handler from Task 4.
- Produces: `soundOn` state persisted to `localStorage['lux-sound']`; `startAudioIfEnabled()` called on first draw gesture.

- [ ] **Step 1: Add audio control to `reading.js`**

Add near the top (after the existing `const` element lookups):

```js
const audio = document.getElementById('bg-audio');
const soundToggle = document.getElementById('sound-toggle');

let soundOn = localStorage.getItem('lux-sound') === 'on';

function reflectSound() {
  soundToggle.textContent = soundOn ? '♪ on' : '♪ off';
  soundToggle.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(soundOn));
  if (soundOn) {
    audio.play().catch(() => {}); // ignored until a user gesture has occurred
  } else {
    audio.pause();
  }
}

soundToggle.addEventListener('click', () => {
  soundOn = !soundOn;
  localStorage.setItem('lux-sound', soundOn ? 'on' : 'off');
  reflectSound();
});

function startAudioIfEnabled() {
  if (soundOn) audio.play().catch(() => {});
}

reflectSound();
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Start audio on the draw gesture**

In the `form` submit handler (Task 4), add `startAudioIfEnabled();` as the first line after `e.preventDefault();`:

```js
form.addEventListener('submit', (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  startAudioIfEnabled();
  currentSpread = drawSpread(DECK);
  // …unchanged…
});
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Manual QA**

Run: reload the page. Click the sound toggle → it reads "♪ on" and music starts (this click is the user gesture). Reload → toggle persists as "♪ on" and, because a gesture is required, music starts on the next interaction (toggle or Draw). With sound on, clicking **Draw** keeps music playing. Toggle to "♪ off" → music pauses.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add reading.js
git commit -m "feat: ambient background music with persisted mute toggle"
```

### Task 6: Groq proxy in Google Apps Script (`docs/apps-script/Code.gs`)

**Files:**
- Create: `docs/apps-script/Code.gs`
- Create: `docs/apps-script/README.md`

**Interfaces:**
- Produces the deployed `/exec` endpoint contract consumed by Task 7: a `POST` with a JSON body `{ type: 'reading', question, cards: [{name, orientation, position, meaning}] }` returns JSON `{ reading: string }`; a body without `type: 'reading'` is treated as a waitlist email (appends a row), preserving existing behavior. Errors return `{ error: string }` with the reading text absent.

- [ ] **Step 1: Create `docs/apps-script/Code.gs`**

```js
// LUX web app: waitlist email capture + Groq tarot reading proxy.
// Deploy: Extensions ▸ Apps Script, paste this, set Script Property GROQ_API_KEY,
// then Deploy ▸ New deployment ▸ Web app ▸ Execute as: Me ▸ Access: Anyone.

function doPost(e) {
  try {
    var body = JSON.parse(e.postData.contents);
    if (body.type === 'reading') {
      return jsonOut({ reading: generateReading(body) });
    }
    appendEmail(body.email);            // existing waitlist behavior
    return jsonOut({ ok: true });
  } catch (err) {
    return jsonOut({ error: String(err && err.message || err) });
  }
}

function appendEmail(email) {
  if (!email) return;
  SpreadsheetApp.getActiveSpreadsheet().getActiveSheet()
    .appendRow([new Date(), email]);
}

function generateReading(body) {
  var key = PropertiesService.getScriptProperties().getProperty('GROQ_API_KEY');
  if (!key) throw new Error('Missing GROQ_API_KEY');

  var cards = (body.cards || []).map(function (c) {
    return '- ' + c.position + ': ' + c.name + ' (' + c.orientation + ') — ' + c.meaning;
  }).join('\n');

  var system =
    'You are LUX, a mystical tarot reader. Voice: evocative, atmospheric, warm but ' +
    'never cheesy. Given a 3-card Past/Present/Future spread, write a cohesive reading ' +
    'of 2-3 short paragraphs. Weave each card\'s position and orientation into a single ' +
    'narrative that speaks to the querent\'s question. Do not list the cards mechanically. ' +
    'End with a single grounded line of guidance. Plain text only.';

  var user = 'Question: ' + (body.question || '(none given)') + '\n\nSpread:\n' + cards;

  var res = UrlFetchApp.fetch('https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/chat/completions', {
    method: 'post',
    contentType: 'application/json',
    headers: { Authorization: 'Bearer ' + key },
    muteHttpExceptions: true,
    payload: JSON.stringify({
      model: 'llama-3.3-70b-versatile',
      temperature: 0.9,
      max_tokens: 500,
      messages: [
        { role: 'system', content: system },
        { role: 'user', content: user },
      ],
    }),
  });

  if (res.getResponseCode() !== 200) {
    throw new Error('Groq ' + res.getResponseCode() + ': ' + res.getContentText());
  }
  var data = JSON.parse(res.getContentText());
  return data.choices[0].message.content.trim();
}

function jsonOut(obj) {
  return ContentService
    .createTextOutput(JSON.stringify(obj))
    .setMimeType(ContentService.MimeType.JSON);
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create `docs/apps-script/README.md`**

```markdown
# LUX Apps Script proxy — setup

1. Open your waitlist Google Sheet ▸ **Extensions ▸ Apps Script**.
2. Replace the script contents with `Code.gs` from this folder.
3. **Project Settings ▸ Script Properties ▸ Add script property**:
   - Name: `GROQ_API_KEY`
   - Value: your key from https://console.groq.com (free tier).
4. **Deploy ▸ Manage deployments ▸ Edit** the existing web app (or **New deployment ▸ Web app**):
   - Execute as: **Me**
   - Who has access: **Anyone**
5. Copy the `/exec` URL. It must match `GROQ_ENDPOINT` in `reading.js`
   (it is the same URL as `SHEETS_ENDPOINT` in `main.js`).

The key lives only in Script Properties — it is never served to the browser.
`llama-3.3-70b-versatile` is a Groq free-tier model; swap the `model` string if Groq
retires it.
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Deploy and smoke-test (manual, performed by the site owner)**

Deploy per the README, then from a terminal:

```bash
curl -s -L -X POST "<YOUR_EXEC_URL>" \
  -H "Content-Type: text/plain" \
  -d '{"type":"reading","question":"test","cards":[{"name":"The Sun","orientation":"upright","position":"Present","meaning":"Joy, success."}]}'
```

Expected: JSON like `{"reading":"..."}` with a few sentences. If you see `{"error":"Missing GROQ_API_KEY"}`, finish step 1's Script Property.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add docs/apps-script/Code.gs docs/apps-script/README.md
git commit -m "feat: add Groq tarot reading proxy for Apps Script"
```

### Task 7: Client AI integration with loading, retry, and fallback (`reading.js`)

**Files:**
- Modify: `reading.js`

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: `currentSpread` and `renderSpread` from Task 4; the endpoint contract from Task 6; `#retry-btn`, `#reading-status`, `#reading` from Task 3.
- Produces: `GROQ_ENDPOINT` constant (same URL as `main.js`'s `SHEETS_ENDPOINT`); `requestReading(spread)` posts to the proxy and renders the text, falling back to `fallbackText` on any failure.

- [ ] **Step 1: Add the endpoint constant and request function to `reading.js`**

Add the constant near the top and the function below `fallbackText`:

```js
const GROQ_ENDPOINT = 'https://script.google.com/macros/s/AKfycbxzK1XY99_2VTiRve-6czO08UdvZ6Ok2VoO8i1_otCeKEEsK2slhzczX8FKs5a2Ba5X/exec';

const retryBtn = document.getElementById('retry-btn');

async function requestReading(spread) {
  statusEl.textContent = 'The cards are speaking…';
  readingEl.textContent = '';
  retryBtn.hidden = true;
  drawBtn.disabled = true;

  const payload = {
    type: 'reading',
    question: questionEl.value.trim(),
    cards: spread.map(s => ({
      name: s.card.name,
      orientation: s.orientation,
      position: s.position,
      meaning: s.orientation === 'reversed' ? s.card.reversed : s.card.upright,
    })),
  };

  try {
    const res = await fetch(GROQ_ENDPOINT, {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain' }, // simple request → no CORS preflight
      body: JSON.stringify(payload),
    });
    const data = await res.json();
    if (!data.reading) throw new Error(data.error || 'No reading returned');
    statusEl.textContent = '';
    renderReading(data.reading);
  } catch (err) {
    statusEl.textContent = 'The connection to the cards faltered — here is what they show plainly.';
    renderReading(fallbackText(spread));
    retryBtn.hidden = false;
  } finally {
    drawBtn.disabled = false;
  }
}

function renderReading(text) {
  readingEl.innerHTML = '';
  text.split(/\n\s*\n/).forEach(para => {
    const p = document.createElement('p');
    p.textContent = para.trim();
    readingEl.appendChild(p);
  });
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Call `requestReading` from the submit handler and wire retry**

Replace the fallback line in the submit handler (`readingEl.textContent = fallbackText(currentSpread);`) with a call to the AI request, and add the retry handler:

```js
form.addEventListener('submit', (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  startAudioIfEnabled();
  currentSpread = drawSpread(DECK);
  renderSpread(currentSpread);
  readingZone.hidden = false;
  requestReading(currentSpread);
});

retryBtn.addEventListener('click', () => requestReading(currentSpread));
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Manual QA**

Run: with the Apps Script deployed (Task 6), reload the page, ask a question, click **Draw**.
Expected: cards flip, "The cards are speaking…" shows, then a 2–3 paragraph reading renders. To verify the fallback: temporarily set `GROQ_ENDPOINT` to an invalid URL, draw, and confirm the curated per-card meanings render with the "connection faltered" note and a working **Retry** button. Restore the real URL afterward.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add reading.js
git commit -m "feat: generate AI readings via Groq proxy with fallback and retry"
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Update `README.md`**

Add this section before `## Deployment`:

```markdown
## Tarot Reading (AI)

`reading.html` is an interactive 3-card (Past / Present / Future) reading. The 78-card
deck lives in `deck.js`; draw logic is in `draw.js` (unit-tested with `node --test test/`).
Readings are generated by Groq (`llama-3.3-70b-versatile`) through the Google Apps Script
web app — the API key stays in Apps Script Script Properties and never reaches the browser.
Setup steps are in `docs/apps-script/README.md`. Ambient music and a mute toggle use the
built-in HTML5 `<audio>` element.

Known limitation: the proxy endpoint is public and consumes the free Groq quota; server-side
rate-limiting is a future hardening step.
```

## Self-Review

**Spec coverage:**
- Separate `reading.html` → Tasks 3, 4, 7. ✓
- 3-card Past/Present/Future spread → Task 2 (`POSITIONS`), Task 4 (render). ✓
- Ask-question-then-draw flow → Tasks 3 (form), 4/7 (submit). ✓
- Reversed cards (logic + 180° rotation) → Task 2 (orientation), Task 4/CSS (`.reversed`). ✓
- Deck: images-only, generated meanings, filename convention → Task 1. ✓
- Card back SVG → already created; referenced in Task 3 CSS. ✓
- Groq via Apps Script, key in Script Properties → Task 6. ✓
- CORS-safe `text/plain` POST, readable JSON → Task 7 (client), Task 6 (`jsonOut`). ✓
- Mystical/evocative medium reading → Task 6 system prompt. ✓
- Ambient audio + mute toggle + localStorage + gesture start → Task 5. ✓
- Error handling: retry + fallback meanings → Task 7. ✓
- Known limitation (public endpoint) documented → Task 6/README Task 8. ✓
- Testing: pure draw logic isolated + manual QA → Tasks 1, 2 (node tests), 3–7 (manual). ✓
- Waitlist page unchanged → only Task 8 adds one link, no behavior change. ✓

**Placeholder scan:** No TBD/TODO; all steps carry concrete code or exact commands. ✓

**Type consistency:** `drawSpread` returns `{card, orientation, position}` used identically in Tasks 4 and 7; `renderSpread`, `fallbackText`, `currentSpread`, `startAudioIfEnabled`, `GROQ_ENDPOINT`, `requestReading`, `renderReading` names are consistent across tasks; card object shape `{id,name,image,upright,reversed}` matches between Task 1 and consumers. ✓
